name: PocketBase settings behavior
pocketBaseNote: PocketBase application settings are superuser-managed after bootstrap, support nested partial patches, redact stored secrets in API responses, and return field-level validation errors for invalid settings.

cases.name: settings defaults are readable before superuser bootstrap
cases.pocketBaseNote: Before the first superuser exists, bootstrap flows can inspect default settings.

cases.name: settings require superuser after bootstrap
cases.pocketBaseNote: After a superuser exists, settings reads and writes require superuser authorization.

cases.name: superuser patch updates settings and redacts secrets
cases.pocketBaseNote: A superuser can patch nested settings sections, but secret values are redacted from the response.

cases.name: redacted secret placeholders preserve stored secrets
cases.pocketBaseNote: Submitting the redaction placeholder for a secret field should keep the previously stored secret value.

cases.name: settings fields projection returns selected settings
cases.pocketBaseNote: Settings responses support fields projection while preserving secret redaction.

cases.name: invalid batch settings return field validation errors
cases.pocketBaseNote: Invalid numeric settings should be rejected with a field-level validation code.

cases.name: enabled backup s3 settings require a secret
cases.pocketBaseNote: Enabled S3-backed backup settings must include all required storage credentials, including the secret.
